findings.rationale: No new trust boundaries introduced. Changes add enforcement gates within the existing policy engine evaluation pipeline. The block condition evaluators operate on pre-collected data (Copilot findings, Dependabot alerts) and do not introduce new external data flows.

evidence.snippet: block condition evaluators consume existing emission data

findings.rationale: STRIDE analysis: no new spoofing, tampering, repudiation, information disclosure, denial of service, or elevation of privilege vectors. The hard gates reduce the attack surface by preventing merges with known vulnerabilities. No new trust boundary crossings.

Attack path analysis: the synthetic emission generation in CI is bounded to the workflow context and cannot be manipulated externally. Block conditions evaluate deterministically against structured data. No bypass vectors identified.

evidence.snippet: synthetic emission generation in CI pre-check step

findings.rationale: Configuration assessment complete. IAM scope unchanged. No new network exposure. CI workflow changes are additive and do not modify existing permissions or secrets handling.
Detection coverage improved by the changes. Hard gates on Copilot findings and Dependabot alerts create observable enforcement points. Governance workflow logs provide audit trail for gate decisions.
ATT&CK technique coverage: T1195 (Supply Chain Compromise) mitigated by Dependabot alert gates. T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter) coverage maintained. No new validation exercises required.
Vulnerability classification complete. No critical or high severity findings. Hard gates improve vulnerability management posture by preventing merge of code with known security issues.
Regulatory impact is positive. Copilot and Dependabot hard gates strengthen SOC 2 Type II (CC7.1), NIST 800-53 SI-2 (Flaw Remediation), and PCI-DSS 6.2 (timely patching) compliance postures.
